name: John Hickenlooper
subject: What I saw in Iowa
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper 2020
body:
What a week it's been! Our Winnebago made nine stops throughout Iowa, finally rolling in to the Iowa State Fair--which I've been wanting to go to for years. | | | [](https://secure.hickenlooper.com/page/m/63771718/691c9bd/6e9cf46a/11a4490b/1206604915/VEsH/?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-130kdonors?refcode=em_template")
---
| Friend,

What a week it's been! Our Winnebago made nine stops throughout Iowa, finally rolling in to the Iowa State Fair--which I've been wanting to go to for years.

**Let me tell you --nothing compares to seeing the festivities in person.** For me, the Iowa State Fair was so much more than a celebration--it was an opportunity to speak to early primary voters who live in both urban and rural communities, who've voted for both Democratic and Republican presidential candidates.

Talking to Iowans from a variety of backgrounds, one commonality came through: voters want effective, realistic solutions to issues like the costs of health care, gun violence in our schools and workplaces, and the security of our elections.

**These are solutions that only an experienced and pragmatic progressive like me can deliver. But we need funding to continue to engage voters at events like this.** [**_Can you chip in now to help me keep going?_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-keep-going?refcode=em_fr_20190813_JH_20190813-fr-JH-statefair-all_all_l1&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=")

As I said on the famous Iowa State Fair soapbox, "When things look the worst, is when we have to work the hardest." I thought about this while visiting some of Iowa's most famous landmarks, like the Field of Dreams, and the workplaces that keep the economy ticking, including an ethanol plant. [](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-keep-going?refcode=em_fr_20190813_JH_20190813-fr-JH-statefair-all_all_i1&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=") [](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-keep-going?refcode=em_fr_20190813_JH_20190813-fr-JH-statefair-all_all_i2&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=") That said, you probably won't be surprised to hear that my favorite part of the fair was grabbing a beer with Iowans and talking about our nation's path forward. [](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-keep-going?refcode=em_fr_20190813_JH_20190813-fr-JH-statefair-all_all_i3&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=") **We have a long 14 months ahead of us, but that 's why we need to dig our heels in now--to spread our vision and build our team.** [**_Can you chip in now to keep this campaign going?_**](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-keep-going?refcode=em_fr_20190813_JH_20190813-fr-JH-statefair-all_all_l2&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=")

Thanks,
John [](https://secure.hickenlooper.?g=Wvb-QcQot1BcsCb456pUfvA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hickenlooper2020-keep-going?refcode=em_fr_20190813_JH_20190813-fr-JH-statefair-all_all_contribbutton&recurring=auto&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&city=Englewood&state=&occupation=&employer=")

name: Robert Foster
subject: Integrity Matters
body:
---
| | | | | | |
---
|
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001bCymoKyNU1OxYsBEv44wda4cn8es0cl8LnPmQQ_wz_upW5N2fZDMABSb86qzkACSVu-r9AI3qA0Xd8tBcb0jfW4YYb6NGJu8YEon3XklxyvSC1c_mpaHobz-f2EPOcq6896MTguFUO3IBzQ1XGMdyE-8aNrhuXh41Tn98Kzr6xN16Qe-TW-wz11RS8m0REUXKl7dQz4f8KI-oIiT47L6ierb9NWovg7m&c=oYHaWBmZ3pgNdC9EVyf-sr0H799m8EJXJikfcdX1pgj-akGy9RLkYg==&ch=SmX1Pa6ezILq2lYlrD8IV5VXfYIc1LiWuqMHqaFX3dJpW6Ney_vK-w==)
---
| |
---
| Gubernatorial runoffs are a rare occurrence in Mississippi's history. In fact, the most recent was nearly three decades ago. Like so many of you, I have struggled to reach a decision on the right plan of action in this runoff election.
For months, I pitched my conservative outsider resume and platform of fresh ideas and a new perspective for our state government. While we were disappointed in the results, we saw that a majority of Mississippians are looking for more than the next-guy-in-line.
We have also seen, at this time in our history, a majority of Mississippians are not happy with the status quo and their vote proved that on Tuesday.
After days of mindful consideration, prayer and soul searching, I have decided that on August 27th, I will cast my ballot for Bill Waller, and I hope you will join me in doing so.
I've come to my decision for a variety of reasons - my conscience being one. For me, this wasn't about who is most conservative or public opinion, but whom I trust to lead our state with integrity, character, and selfless service - traits I have witnessed first-hand in Brigadier General Waller over the course of this election.
This Integrity matters, and it provides a significant reason to cast your ballot for Bill Waller.
Second, policy. Even though we don't always get what we want in a candidate, I know, without a doubt, that Bill Waller will do what's in the best interest of all Mississippians - NOT special interests and lobbyist.
For anyone concerned with the issues - I hope you will look at his position thoroughly instead of political sound bites or deceiving commercials. Medicaid is not a program we need to expand. It is a program we need to reform.
Third, November. Jim Hood is wholly unacceptable - by any measure. If he wins in November, our state will - without a doubt - be subjected to left-wing policies for generations to come. Justice Waller has proven time and again that he can beat Hood in November, and he is the only candidate standing in his way of turning our state BLUE.
My conscience tells me to do what I can to ensure that doesn't happen. Fourth, solutions. For some time, our third candidate has spent a considerable amount of time discussing national issues, while ignoring the challenges we face in Mississippi - like teacher pay and shortage, a rising opioid crisis, and infrastructure. Bill Waller, in contrast, has offered real, long-term solutions to our biggest problems facing Mississippi families, and I encourage you to do your research.
Instead of fighting over political ideologies, we have to come together to find common ground on the issues we face together. I am confident in the republican state legislature, who controls legislation, that, together, they will bring about conservative solutions in a way that doesn't put taxpayers or our state budget at risk.
Finally, I have never needed approval to vote my conscience, and neither do you. I didn't become one of the most conservative house members by selling out my convictions, and I am not going to start now. Our shared values are too important to sit idly by on the sidelines and spectate.
In December, I committed to do what's in the best interest of all Mississippians, and I intend on keeping that commitment.
In the end, we each just have one vote, or we can stay home. But if you don't want to see Hood win in November, I encourage you to join me in voting for Bill Waller.
With only 13 days left until the runoff, Justice Waller needs our help to be successful on August 27th.
If you would like to get involved in their campaign or get a yard sign, [visit the Waller website here](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001bCymoKyNU1OxYsBEv44wda4cn8es0cl8LnPmQQ_wz_upW5N2fZDMAF8NnXme2fRsFPdmwmENqTeHn7Hhaf3kGfWui6PQKhyGrc6Dzzym-Iam9Ozs58jtjSFbW1LpI6GmIGRbXjZiNTnvP7q7OZjhWUMj8yYPSUw8&c=oYHaWBmZ3pgNdC9EVyf-sr0H799m8EJXJikfcdX1pgj-akGy9RLkYg==&ch=SmX1Pa6ezILq2lYlrD8IV5VXfYIc1LiWuqMHqaFX3dJpW6Ney_vK-w==)
Or, please consider making financial contribution to the Waller campaign before the runoff, [you can donate securely online here](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001bCymoKyNU1OxYsBEv44wda4cn8es0cl8LnPmQQ_wz_upW5N2fZDMAF8NnXme2fRs6DCVmMz3r7y9XSocBph8jRt9HG05CsV-xIZfwk1yHJ_RGNm8TiyF-6Bmsn2hivh4c-ubbcvBxNBVdkuBh46FdNh1Vam4xJHdlXgOhxC7hX3kjD73Ucy9hQ==&c=oYHaWBmZ3pgNdC9EVyf-sr0H799m8EJXJikfcdX1pgj-akGy9RLkYg==&ch=SmX1Pa6ezILq2lYlrD8IV5VXfYIc1LiWuqMHqaFX3dJpW6Ney_vK-w==)

subject: Tim's vision
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for America
body:
|
---
Hey there, folks.

While campaign life can get exhausting, it's also incredibly rewarding to be able to travel and talk to folks about what they want out of a President -- especially leading up to the most important election of our lives.

That's why Tim's so excited to make it out to talk to voters right away. [**Will you help us keep Tim on the road talking to voters in the early states?**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_1_1&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+Will+you+help+us+keep+Tim+on+the+road+ta&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_1_1&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_1_1")

So far, it's clear that working families are on board with Tim's vision for new and better ideas for America -- including transforming our health care system, investing in education, protecting our unions, and finally investing in our communities again.

This is what Tim's plan is all about -- equipping families and communities with the opportunities promised by the American Dream -- [**but we 're heading to New Hampshire, South Carolina and Iowa again soon, and I'm hearing that all this on the ground work has left our fundraising depleted.**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_1_1&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=2+-+but+wersquore+heading+to+New+Hampshire+S&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_1_1&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_1_1")

[**Can you help us by chipping in $3 so that Tim has what he needs to keep making his way around these great united states?**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_1_1&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=3+-+Can+you+help+us+by+chipping+in+ask+so+th&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_1_1&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_1_1")

Andrea

| | | | | | [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001rN_wGpsxcQxn7sJPaapjQ9aOMxwKI5fpxXKzd51ldq3HDB6f4UQtzRZxn790sSUhG0fa1rVrUyGkPXC-sXu955eeJt6N4hKcCm_l6bF68Z9G9pZXKNLaraLaCDoqipamtgF8ie4cIDYSz7VoM40X6Q==&c=0gmvOprRPOCuFv3cFVw33MoYh0LezenCCo3XTDcK-eEmxRjM6jdzTg==&ch=2h15sztjRkeJ66WczCDNHPnBsGw436bUobHcgzWEtoCoB8_bqzlNbw==)

| Dear Friend,
I'm disappointed, but not surprised, that my opponent and his Washington Political Machine are lying about my record. Instead of bringing people together to solve problems, Max, Nancy Pelosi and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez have spent the last seven months pushing their partisan agenda.
You may have seen a new digital ad against me funded by the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee. Not only does it lie about my position on health care reform, but it ridiculously tries to connect me with a lawsuit filed by Attorneys General in other states.
Let me be clear. I support protections for those with preexisting conditions. If Max and his political machine were being honest, they would tell you that in the State Assembly I voted to make it illegal to deny coverage to any person with preexisting conditions.
I've also voted to lower prescription drug costs by taking a hard line against price gouging and voted to strengthen our system of employer-based health insurance, while protecting Medicare for seniors and providing quality coverage to Americans in need.

Instead of bringing people together to solve problems, Max, Nancy Pelosi and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez have spent the last seven months pushing their partisan agenda. You may have seen a new digital ad against me funded by the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee. Not only does it lie about my position on health care reform, but it ridiculously tries to connect me with a lawsuit filed by Attorneys General in other states. Let me be clear. I support protections for those with preexisting conditions. name: Megan West
subject: INTERVIEW: Is there hope for the next generation? This Pastor explains
body:
| | A thought-provoking Interview with Pastor Jonathan Pokluda
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=jalJvum2JQxpPvfjGmN5k22ygf7T2ySz1nt4EaOaBSU&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190814com01&n=1)
| Peter, Do you ever find yourself worried about Millennials and Generation Z? Do you wonder if the next generation will be able to overcome apathy and entitlement? Or maybe you are part of the Millennial Generation or Generation Z, and you have experienced the disconnect between your generation and the ones above you. **I sat down for a**[**fascinating conversation**](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=jalJvum2JQxpPvfjGmN5k22ygf7T2ySz1nt4EaOaBSU&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190814com01&n=2)**with Pastor Jonathan Pokluda from Harris Creek Church in Waco, Texas.** **Pastor Pokluda, the author of _Welcome to Adulting_ , sheds light on the struggles the next generation is facing and explains how they can find life and find it abundantly. ** We’ve said it before, but it’s worth mentioning again; Millennials and Generation Z will be the largest voting bloc in 2020. This means, not only will they influence elections, they lead our culture. To advance a biblical worldview, we must first understand the next generation and then equip them with truth to make a difference. Pastor Pokluda helps explain how we can do just that. “Millennials underestimate what they can do with long obedience in the same direction. I think they overestimate what they can do in the short term. And so what they are not doing well is patience, but what they are doing well is belief — believing that they can change the world. And if they take that belief and place it in something bigger than themselves — they begin to understand the purpose of why they were created. Then I believe the Holy Spirit, God, partners with them and shows them how they actually can.” – Pastor Jonathan Pokluda [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=jalJvum2JQxpPvfjGmN5k22ygf7T2ySz1nt4EaOaBSU&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190814com01&n=3) [**Watch our interview**](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=jalJvum2JQxpPvfjGmN5k22ygf7T2ySz1nt4EaOaBSU&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190814com01&n=4) and be encouraged and challenged by what he has to say about the next generation, the church, and our culture today. Pray. Think. Vote. Megan West
Media Director
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190814com01&n=5)

| Peter,

Self-funding candidate and billionaire Tom Steyer, after spending nearly $10 million on advertising over the past few weeks, just announced he's within striking distance of securing a spot on the September debate stage. He crossed the 130,000-donor threshold and is just one poll away from meeting all of the DNC's qualification requirements.

If he qualifies, he'll be the 10th candidate guaranteed a spot on stage, alongside Joe Biden, Cory Booker, Pete Buttigieg, Kamala Harris, Amy Klobuchar, Beto O'Rourke, Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, and Andrew Yang.

We're getting closer to securing my spot, but we're not quite there yet, and time is running out.

name: Dan Baer
subject: The climate crisis is a national security crisis.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Baer for Colorado
body:
Conventional wisdom says voters don't care about foreign policy. But conventional wisdom is wrong.
|
---
[ ](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82791/232dfd1/9ebb0dc/62a13a26/3069685335/VEsH/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://www.danforcolorado.com")

| | Conventional wisdom says voters don't care about foreign policy. But conventional wisdom is wrong. Because foreign policy touches most everything we Coloradans worry about here at home.

Most career politicians lack foreign policy experience, and many fail to communicate how what happens beyond our borders affects our communities here at home.

I'm not a career politician, I'm a Colorado native and former U.S. ambassador who represented our country and President Obama overseas. I was part of the team spanning the White House, State Department, USAID, and Defense department that helped set U.S. foreign policy aimed at delivering results for all 330 million Americans - so I'll give it a shot.

Put simply: bad foreign policy decisions aren't just felt in Beijing or London or Mexico City, they hurt Coloradans. Bad foreign policy decisions make it harder to earn a living and live our lives the way we want to here in Colorado.

Let's use the global climate crisis as an example. We're now two years out from President Trump's decision to withdraw the United States from the Paris Agreement, a deal that put the rest of the world to work to limit carbon emissions and avoid the most disastrous effects of a warming planet.

From a foreign policy perspective, the Paris Agreement was important because it put the United States in the position of helping to set the framework for cooperation that other countries - including China and India - also agreed to. In the foreign policy world, getting those countries to agree to standards at the negotiating table is considered a huge win. It's also a win that would have been felt at home and in Colorado.

The United States may be out of the Paris Agreement today, but it lives on - the difference is that other countries now run the show and build out the rules. Meanwhile, the United States continues to step backwards in our efforts to stem the worst impacts of the climate crisis, and that will have disastrous effects for Coloradans.

Already, climate change contributes to decreased snowpack and shorter ski seasons; lower river levels that shut down rafting trips in Durango and elsewhere; drought and habitat loss leading to elk herd die-offs that affect Colorado's hunting season, despite the best efforts of conservationists and sportswomen and men; and warming lakes that threaten 147 freshwater fish species, which will mean less action for Colorado anglers.

In Colorado, outdoor recreation is central to our identity.

Our use of shared spaces gives us a sense of common purpose and is part of our way of life. Climate change - and the potential for the Paris Agreement to undo its damage - may feel abstract, but we feel its impacts in our hearts and at home. Outdoor recreation is part of why we love living here, and it's how many Coloradans earn a living.

According to a 2018 report, Colorado's outdoor industry contributes $62.5 billion to the state's GDP and supports more than half a million jobs. That's nearly one-fifth of Colorado's economy in terms of both total economic output and employment, an industry that's growing each year, and that can't be outsourced.

When one out of every five Coloradans depends on a job that relies on the health of our skiing, rafting, hunting, fishing and other outdoor industries to earn a paycheck and put food on the table, it makes sense that voters are increasingly concerned about climate change, and a 2019 poll backs this up.

If voters care about climate change, they care about foreign policy, too. Rejoining the Paris Agreement, or negotiating a "Paris plus" agreement, would put other countries to work protecting hundreds of thousands of jobs and hundreds of small businesses here in Colorado.

Colorado voters should elect leaders who have that experience and can hold the White House accountable for putting us back on the right foreign policy path - not for the sake of global diplomacy but to protect and preserve our way of life.

_If you also believe that climate change is a national security issue and that we need more public servants with foreign policy backgrounds in the Senate, please share[the published version of this op-ed](https://go.danforcolorado.com/page/m/5fb82791/232dfd1/9ebb0dc/62a13a27/3069685335/VEsE/?g=JnF4Cq_HJ6FcyTX3h0hkK5A "https://durangoherald.com/articles/289214"), which first ran in The Durango Herald on 8/10, on social media with your friends and family._

Thanks!
\--Dan

Hi Peter, On Monday, the Trump Administration announced they were going to weaken the Endangered Species Act. 2. "Economic assessments" are now allowed to undermine scientific evidence and determine whether or not an animal is in harm's way.
3. This is a clear path for drilling and mining to increase in areas where now-classified endangered species live.

When I took office in January, I promised to do my part to protect our environment. The future our children inherit is determined by the actions we take now.

Please stand with me today and help save the Endangered Species Act. [_**Click here to show your support for the Endangered Species Act >>**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/YTWVI6dq6Xec6fOCtOzqNyOGsb_TFaCAI1i-Kmy8vqM=/rFDFy0OG5l_7jetOIi71vl3pphDyQ1Jh7qa8O9AIzmU=) Thank you, _-Abigail_

subject: Montana needs real representation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by A whole lot of folks for Wilmot Collins, PO Box 458, Helena, MT 59624
body:
#
Team,
You may have heard me say that Montana only has one Senator. That's because the people of Montana can only count on Jon Tester to fight for them in the Senate.
Steve Daines not only obeys the strict GOP party line, he supports and parrots some of President Trump's most vile attacks and ideals.
That's not how real Montanans act. We believe in working hard and working together to achieve our goals and truly making our great state the last best place.
[**Montana needs two Senators representing us. Contribute $4.06 to help make it happen.**](https://act.wilmotcollins.?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190814&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
My opponent, Steve Daines, won't even hold a town hall to listen to the thoughts and concerns of Montanans.
I've traveled to all corners of Montana and listened to the people and will continue to do so in order to truly be the voice of Montanans. That is the kind of Senator the people of this state need--someone who will meet with them, listen to them, and make their issues his own.
[**Please help support our campaign to bring sanity and good government back to Washington. Help give Montanans two Senators who will fight for them by donating $4.06 today.**](https://act.wilmotcollins.?g=ENsuPVNw52hc6i6rfY8XtkA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wc-email?refcode=em190814&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Thank you,
Wilmot

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Dick Durbin

#
[**Sean Casten, Betsy Dirksen Londrigan, and Lauren Underwood are fighting to keep Illinois blue. Contribute $5 now to help fund their 2020 campaigns.**](http://action.dickdurbin.?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-house-slate-august-2019?refcode=em190814&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Peter,
In 2018, we made history when we flipped formerly Republican districts and elected Democrats Sean Casten and Lauren Underwood to fight for the people of Illinois. And we almost elected Betsy Dirksen Londrigan as well, who missed beating an incumbent Republican by _**less than one percent**_.
In 2020, we need to defend and build on our progress. It's going to take all of us standing together to reelect Sean and Lauren and to help put Betsy over the top in the next election.
[**Can these Democratic candidates count on your support in the fight to keep Illinois blue? Pitch in $5 to Sean Casten, Betsy Dirksen Londrigan, and Lauren Underwood now.**](http://action.dickdurbin.?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-house-slate-august-2019?refcode=em190814&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
In the House, Sean and Lauren have been standing up for people in Illinois and the nation, fighting to defend affordable health care, raise the minimum wage, and act on climate.
Betsy is ready to replace a Republican rubber stamp for President Trump and join Sean and Lauren in these fights--but she can only win if we all stand with her and support her now.
[**Help keep Illinois blue: contribute $5 to Sean, Betsy, and Lauren 's campaigns now.**](http://action.dickdurbin.?g=ofPncVH8x8VcsCmaF7wmZzA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dd-house-slate-august-2019?refcode=em190814&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=34223&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&addr1=&addr2=&city=Englewood&state=FL&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
My best,
Dick Durbin

name: Chirlane McCray, de Blasio 2020
subject: Victory is sweet
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DE BLASIO 2020
body:
There's nothing like reminding folks to never underestimate you.
# [](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21ffb/1630b3eb/510d8576/5f554617/442168630/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjcwNSJ9/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/de_blasio_donate_now?refcode=705_org_em_fr")
Friend --
It's Wednesday, and I'm still thinking about last weekend! Bill, Dante, and I traveled across the state of Iowa talking to working people about everything from mental health to gun reform. We met some incredible people and made some incredible family memories along the way, too.
**[Check out this video of the three of us first arriving at that great traditional campaign stop: the Iowa State Fair.](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21ffb/1630b3eb/510d8576/5f554616/442168630/VEsE/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://twitter.com/BilldeBlasio/status/1160633167587487751")**
You see, Sunday was our first time at the Iowa State Fair. Bill challenged Dante to a match at various "games of skill," betting on a quick and easy defeat.
[](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21ffb/1630b3eb/510d8576/5f554615/442168630/VEsF/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://twitter.com/Chirlane/status/1160656930001625089?s=20")
Little did they know, I was the one they should have been watching out for! From basketball to skeeball, I took home not one, but TWO State Fair wins!
But the real victory was just getting to connect with so many people in such a fun, informal setting. That kind of deeper connection goes a long way toward building a network in this influential early state.
**[Share this video of me beating both Bill and Dante. Priceless!](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21ffb/1630b3eb/510d8576/5f554614/442168630/VEsC/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://twitter.com/Chirlane/status/1160645807319801857")**
Don't get me wrong: Dante and Bill had their share of victories too, but there's nothing like reminding folks to never underestimate you.
That's what every single campaign we've led has been about. And thanks to your support, I'm confident we can keep showing the world what it looks like to win.
Here's to making more memories along the way!
Chirlane

[CONTRIBUTE»](https://act.billdeblasio.com/page/m/6bf21ffb/1630b3eb/510d8576/5f554617/442168630/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjcwNSJ9/?g=VmvkeFNw52hdLWhQmfUOXQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/de_blasio_donate_now?refcode=705_org_em_fr")

| | | We wanted to make sure you saw this important survey that Francis sent about the crisis of rising drug prices. We really care about your input, and want to hear directly from you about what we can do together to lower costs.

[**Will you take our survey right away?**](https://conole.bsd.?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://conole.bsd.net/page/s/survey-drug-prices?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=1+-+Will+you+take+our+survey+right+away&utm_campaign=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3&source=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3&refcode=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3")

Thanks,

Team Conole

** _Begin forwarded message:_**

We live in a country where good people needlessly suffer because of soaring health insurance and drug costs. **It 's no secret why: drug companies invest millions in politicians like John Katko to stop anyone from lower drug costs and eating into their profits.**

Unlike Katko, we value what's best for people not what's profitable for drug companies. That's why we are reaching out to ask for your opinion.

[**Click here to let us know what you think about drug companies buying politicians like John Katko to keep prescription drug prices high:**](https://conole.bsd.?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://conole.bsd.net/page/s/survey-drug-prices?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=2+-+Click+here+to+let+us+know+what+you+think&utm_campaign=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3&source=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3&refcode=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3") [Drug prices are too high](https://conole.bsd.?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://conole.bsd.net/page/s/survey-drug-prices?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=3+-+Drug+prices+are+too+high&utm_campaign=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3&source=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3&refcode=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3") We already know where Katko stands -- he voted against reforms that would have limited the power of health insurance and drug companies to influence our government.

**_Corporate backed politicians like John Katko aren 't going to stand up to the drug companies. But we will!_**

To lower drug prices, it's going to take all of us coming together and organizing a grassroots movement. That's why I'm reaching out to ask for your input.

[**Answer our drug prices survey now and let us know what you think about rising prices!**](https://conole.bsd.?g=G-UoSFH8x8Vc1mgFDtC1r5A "https://conole.bsd.net/page/s/survey-drug-prices?firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=conole&utm_content=4+-+Answer+our+drug+prices+survey+now+and+le&utm_campaign=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3&source=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3&refcode=FC_EM_SV_90DO_190814_2_3")

Yours in service,

Francis

name: Senator Jeff Merkley
subject: I want to tell you about my dad
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
| [](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2daaa/6cd27893/50c7e196/1580d30f/3628420893/VEsH/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "http://www.jeffmerkley.com")
---

| | I know the importance of a solid, steady paycheck. Growing up, my dad was a union mechanic. On his wages, my parents were able to buy a modest ranch home, take annual camping vacations, and save a little for retirement. Thanks to my dad and mom's hard work -- and some hard work by some excellent teachers -- I was able to be the first in my family to go to college. And I was determined to pass along that work ethic to my children -- inspired by the example mom and dad set in their lives. That's why I'm writing to you today. Because I need to make something perfectly clear: when we talk about our clean energy future, this isn't only about saving our planet. This is about prosperity for our families and for our future. About good jobs. **The transition to a clean energy economy and away from fossil fuels is already happening across the world. How we act now will determine whether America leads that transition, creating millions of good-paying, family-wage jobs.** Or whether that opportunity, and those jobs, will belong to some other country. With the right investment in a clean energy economy, more and more families will be able to lift themselves up and thrive. By ensuring that more clean energy jobs are union jobs and provide good wages and working conditions, we can guarantee better lives and better futures for so many of our friends and neighbors. **Now it's on _us_. Do we make the smart investment in our future, or do we double down on the polluting ways of the past that are already endangering our planet?** [**We need a Senate that knows we must act now. Chip in today and let's get it done!**](https://secure.jeffmerkley.com/page/m/16c2daaa/6cd27893/50c7e196/1580d30c/3628420893/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ==/?g=fjbuEq_HJ6FcsCg1a40jRRA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-jeff-merkley?refcode=2019-08-13-cleanenergy&recurring=1&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") I'm doing my part in the Senate by championing legislation that will move us into the future with a strong clean energy economy; legislation that ensures that when we create clean energy jobs that more of those jobs are family-wage jobs with strong protections for workers. The next step is on our movement. This will only be possible if we take back the Senate from the anti-science, anti-clean energy obstructionists like Mitch McConnell -- who are all too happy to take big campaign dollars from polluters even if it means sacrificing our future. clean_body: | "" | | I know the importance of a solid, steady paycheck. Growing up, my dad was a union mechanic. On his wages, my parents were able to buy a modest ranch home, take annual camping vacations, and save a little for retirement. Thanks to my dad and mom's hard work -- and some hard work by some excellent teachers -- I was able to be the first in my family to go to college. And I was determined to pass along that work ethic to my children -- inspired by the example mom and dad set in their lives. That's why I'm writing to you today. Because I need to make something perfectly clear: when we talk about our clean energy future, this isn't only about saving our planet. This is about prosperity for our families and for our future. About good jobs. The transition to a clean energy economy and away from fossil fuels is already happening across the world. How we act now will determine whether America leads that transition, creating millions of good-paying, family-wage jobs. Or whether that opportunity, and those jobs, will belong to some other country. With the right investment in a clean energy economy, more and more families will be able to lift themselves up and thrive. By ensuring that more clean energy jobs are union jobs and provide good wages and working conditions, we can guarantee better lives and better futures for so many of our friends and neighbors. Now it's on us. Do we make the smart investment in our future, or do we double down on the polluting ways of the past that are already endangering our planet? "" I'm doing my part in the Senate by championing legislation that will move us into the future with a strong clean energy economy; legislation that ensures that when we create clean energy jobs that more of those jobs are family-wage jobs with strong protections for workers. The next step is on our movement. This will only be possible if we take back the Senate from the anti-science, anti-clean energy obstructionists like Mitch McConnell -- who are all too happy to take big campaign dollars from polluters even if it means sacrificing our future. "" Onward. name: James Mackler
subject: All the wrong reasons
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James Mackler for U
body:
Tennessee is making national news for all the wrong reasons all over again.
|
---
[ ](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa273/18d6a294/4b349e63/26d66e96/288899131/VEsH/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://www.jamesmackler.com/")

| | | | "This abortion bill is probably unconstitutional. A Republican lawmaker says that's the point." _Washington Post_ (8/12/19)
---
Tennessee is making national news for all the wrong reasons all over again.
The gerrymandered supermajority in our state legislature is _still_ trying to take away women's constitutional rights.
[**_Tell them to stop the attacks on women 's reproductive freedom. Add your name._**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa273/18d6a294/4b349e63/26d66e97/288899131/VEsE/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190814")
We've been down this road already this year. One of my opponents even recorded an ad to support the same type of heartbeat bill passed in Alabama. While that bill was defeated, those same folks are back again **with one of the most restrictive abortion laws in the country.**
This is dangerous and wrong, and it can't stand.
These attacks on women's healthcare and rights will put lives at risk and restrict a decision that no politician should have anything to do with.
The legal challenges to this knowingly unconstitutional bill are going to waste taxpayer dollars that could be better spent promoting policies that benefit Tennessee women and families, like access to healthcare and keeping our communities safe from gun violence.
[**_We need to stand with Tennessee women and help protect their rights. Join me and add your name today._**](https://secure.jamesmackler.com/page/m/364aa273/18d6a294/4b349e63/26d66e97/288899131/VEsF/?g=HpMoNK0LBwxckIwjLS9nKqA "https://secure.jamesmackler.com/e190814")
Thank you,
James

|
---

---

Hi Team,

Wanted to bump Andrea's message --

We need to win the early states in order to keep up our momentum -- but we urgently need the resources to keep making our case to undecided voters.

[**That 's where you come in -- can you chip in $3 or whatever you can to help us spread our message and secure the Democratic nomination once and for all?**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=1+-+Thatrsquos+where+you+come+in+mdash+can+y&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2")

This is important, team. First the primaries, then the nomination, and FINALLY the White House (in that order).

Let's do it!

[**https://secure.timryanforamerica.com/travel-fund**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=2+-+httpssecuretimryanforamericacomtravelfun&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2")

**Begin Forwarded Message:**

> Hey there, folks.
>
> While campaign life can get exhausting, it's also incredibly rewarding to be able to travel and talk to folks about what they want out of a President -- especially leading up to the most important election of our lives.
>
> That's why Tim's so excited to make it out to talk to voters right away. [**Will you help us keep Tim on the road talking to voters in the early states?**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=3+-+Will+you+help+us+keep+Tim+on+the+road+ta&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2")
>
> So far, it's clear that working families are on board with Tim's vision for new and better ideas for America -- including transforming our health care system, investing in education, protecting our unions, and finally investing in our communities again.
>
> This is what Tim's plan is all about -- equipping families and communities with the opportunities promised by the American Dream -- [**but we 're heading to New Hampshire, South Carolina and Iowa again soon, and I'm hearing that all this on the ground work has left our fundraising depleted.**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=4+-+but+wersquore+heading+to+New+Hampshire+S&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2")
>
> [**Can you help us by chipping in $3 so that Tim has what he needs to keep making his way around these great united states?**](http://secure.timryanforamerica.?g=i0wvQ1H8x8Va89sNxWeKKQ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tr-august-earlystates?refcode=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&amount=3&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&addr1=&addr2=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=&occupation=&employer=&utm_medium=email&utm_source=timryan&utm_content=5+-+Can+you+help+us+by+chipping+in+ask+so+th&utm_campaign=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2&source=TR_EM_FR_ACT_190814_2_2")
>
> Thanks for always coming through for us, team!

> Andrea

subject: Flipping the Virginia Senate together - Step 2
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | | **This is the second part of a series on incredible Democratic candidates running to flip Virginia 's General Assembly in 2019. This email features two strong leaders: [Missy Cotter Smasal](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1763/3e0906/78e5804e/3e30ef51/4199680207/VEsH/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-missy-cotter-smasal?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+Missy+Cotter+Smasal&utm_campaign=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2&source=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2") and [Delegate Cheryl Turpin](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1763/3e0906/78e5804e/3e30ef50/4199680207/VEsE/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-cheryl-turpin?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+Delegate+Cheryl+Turpin&utm_campaign=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2&source=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2") in Virginia Beach. **
---

Derek:
This November, the eyes of the nation will once again turn to the Commonwealth of Virginia as voters go to the polls to elect our next state legislature. We're so often a bellwether for what's to come across the country. Just remember how we kicked off the blue wave with historic elections in 2017! Two years later, I'm inspired by the huge number of amazing people -- many of them activists, organizers, and longtime fighters for good -- who have stepped up to run for office in hopes of flipping our General Assembly in 2019. They know, like you and I do, that a Democratic majority will lead to important progress for Virginia families. So when the pundits zero in on Virginia in less than three months, let's make sure we're ready to show them the best of who we are.

_**Here are two of those inspiring people are running for State Senate in 2019. I hope you 'll read a little more about them and consider joining me in supporting their campaigns: **_

**Missy Cotter Smasal, running for State Senate in District 8:**
Missy Cotter Smasal's life has always been guided by her passion for service and leadership. She served as an officer in the United States Navy and owned and operated a successful small business. She's a dedicated parent, coach, and volunteer, and has advocated for safety in Virginia Beach schools. And today, she's running to be a Virginia state senator. If elected, Missy will work within the community to build a safe, environmentally resilient, and economically strong Virginia Beach. **I hope you 'll join me in enthusiastically supporting her candidacy.** [**Help make sure she has the resources she needs to win in November by making a donation to her campaign today.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1763/3e0906/78e5804e/3e30ef57/4199680207/VEsF/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-missy-cotter-smasal?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=3+-+Help+make+sure+she+has+the+resources+she&utm_campaign=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2&source=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2")
******[**DONATE TO MISSY COTTER SMASAL**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1763/3e0906/78e5804e/3e30ef56/4199680207/VEsC/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-missy-cotter-smasal?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=4+-+DONATE+TO+MISSY+COTTER+SMASAL&utm_campaign=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2&source=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2")******
---

**Cheryl Turpin, running for State Senate in District 7:**
Cheryl Turpin grew up in a military family that settled in Fairfax, Virginia. She's worked as a science teacher in Virginia Beach for 24 years, most recently teaching Environmental Studies at Frank W. Cox High School. As an educator and a concerned citizen, she decided to run for office to work towards a Virginia with better access to health care, stronger schools, and a renewed commitment to protecting our environment. As State Delegate, she's done exactly that -- and now she's taking the fight to the State Senate, running to represent District 7 and help flip the General Assembly. **I believe in Cheryl, I 'm proud to support her candidacy, and** [**I hope you 'll join me by chipping in to her campaign here.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1763/3e0906/78e5804e/3e30ef55/4199680207/VEsD/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-cheryl-turpin?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=5+-+I+hope+yoursquoll+join+me+by+chipping+in&utm_campaign=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2&source=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2")
******[**DONATE TO CHERYL TURPIN**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1763/3e0906/78e5804e/3e30ef54/4199680207/VEsA/?g=_j7eKVH8x8VayGMy1IzDoYA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-cheryl-turpin?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=6+-+DONATE+TO+CHERYL+TURPIN&utm_campaign=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2&source=20190814_4_FR_TK_FTS2")******
---

Thank you for taking the time to learn a little about these two candidates. I hope you're as fired up as I am about their commitment to public service and dedication to making Virginia the best it can be. Our national politics are important -- and next year we'll work hard to keep our hard-won House seats, re-elect my friend Mark Warner, and put a Democrat in the White House. But before we do any of that, we have a chance right now to secure critical Democratic victories and flip Virginia's state legislature blue.

Thanks for all that you do,

-Tim Kaine

clean_body: | | | This is the second part of a series on incredible Democratic candidates running to flip Virginia 's General Assembly in 2019. This email features two strong leaders: "" and "" in Virginia Beach. Derek: This November, the eyes of the nation will once again turn to the Commonwealth of Virginia as voters go to the polls to elect our next state legislature. We're so often a bellwether for what's to come across the country. Just remember how we kicked off the blue wave with historic elections in 2017! Two years later, I'm inspired by the huge number of amazing people -- many of them activists, organizers, and longtime fighters for good -- who have stepped up to run for office in hopes of flipping our General Assembly in 2019. They know, like you and I do, that a Democratic majority will lead to important progress for Virginia families. So when the pundits zero in on Virginia in less than three months, let's make sure we're ready to show them the best of who we are. Here are two of those inspiring people are running for State Senate in 2019. I hope you 'll read a little more about them and consider joining me in supporting their campaigns: Missy Cotter Smasal, running for State Senate in District 8: Missy Cotter Smasal's life has always been guided by her passion for service and leadership. She served as an officer in the United States Navy and owned and operated a successful small business. She's a dedicated parent, coach, and volunteer, and has advocated for safety in Virginia Beach schools. And today, she's running to be a Virginia state senator. If elected, Missy will work within the community to build a safe, environmentally resilient, and economically strong Virginia Beach. I hope you 'll join me in enthusiastically supporting her candidacy. "" **** ""** Cheryl Turpin, running for State Senate in District 7: Cheryl Turpin grew up in a military family that settled in Fairfax, Virginia. She's worked as a science teacher in Virginia Beach for 24 years, most recently teaching Environmental Studies at Frank W. Cox High School. As an educator and a concerned citizen, she decided to run for office to work towards a Virginia with better access to health care, stronger schools, and a renewed commitment to protecting our environment. As State Delegate, she's done exactly that -- and now she's taking the fight to the State Senate, running to represent District 7 and help flip the General Assembly. I believe in Cheryl, I 'm proud to support her candidacy, and "" ** ""** Thank you for taking the time to learn a little about these two candidates. I hope you're as fired up as I am about their commitment to public service and dedication to making Virginia the best it can be. Our national politics are important -- and next year we'll work hard to keep our hard-won House seats, re-elect my friend Mark Warner, and put a Democrat in the White House. But before we do any of that, we have a chance right now to secure critical Democratic victories and flip Virginia's state legislature blue. Thanks for all that you do, -Tim Kaine | "" "" ""

| | Peter,

While most of our team is out knocking on doors and talking to voters across Kentucky, I've been here in campaign HQ crunching the numbers. Math may not be the most exciting part of a campaign, but when it comes to tracking the progress of our field program, it's essential.

This race is close -- **_Cook Political Report_ has marked it a toss-up** \-- and the strength of our field team could make the difference. With each door they knock, and every voter they speak to, they move the needle closer to victory -- one Kentuckian at a time.

We can't slow down at all if we want to ensure Andy wins in November. And that means we need everyone to pitch in.

Good field programs are super effective, but they aren't cheap. [**Donate today to keep us on track to fund this vital program!**](https://action.andybeshear.com/page/m/52557af7/ad6dc73/1496c29c/db42a0f/1742323363/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjE5OCJ9/?g=8QZoJjpTVzRcx_d4IN6n_jA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ab-fieldfund?refcode=20190814nd__198__&amount=5")

**We have already knocked on more than 100,000 doors, but with Election Day less than three months away, we can 't slow down now.** And to keep going at this speed, we need your help:
* $5 will help us buy the supplies we need for one canvasser (clipboard, water, pencils, walking maps)
* $10 will help cover gas we need to drop off volunteers in communities around the commonwealth
* $20 will help us fund more canvasser trainings so we can get even more volunteers out on the street talking to Kentuckians

**So no matter the amount, please take a moment to make a donation and help us keep our field program going.** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

#
We all knew Stacey Abrams wasn’t going anywhere. **She’s still out there, and she’s doing everything she can to deliver Georgia for national Democrats in 2020.**

**Here’s what we’ve learned in recent weeks:**
* Stacey Abrams has raised at least $4 million this year – after raising $27 million last year.
* She is training hundreds of so-called “warriors” for her field team in Georgia.
* She is replicating her successful model nationally in states around the country.

**For Stacey Abrams, the goal is simple: deliver the White House and U.S. Senate majority for Democrats, so they can implement their socialist agenda. **

**Georgia is on the front lines of this battle.** It’s Stacey Abrams’s top priority. Her political machine has already held dozens of events around the state and they won't be stopping anytime soon. **They are working overtime to turn Georgia into California. **

On top of all of this, Abrams continues to receive constant attention from the national liberal media. **They know that if she delivers Georgia, Democrats win the White House and Senate.**

**Between now and November 2020, we cannot let Stacey Abrams and her political machine go unchallenged.**

We have to respond by building the strongest grassroots team in Georgia history. We have to reach as many Georgians as possible with our powerful message of results. We have to continue exposing radical Democrats for their support of socialist policies that are proven to fail.

**I know we can** **win. **I know we can stop Stacey Abrams once and for all. That's why I need your continued help and support. I cannot do this without you.

Thank you for everything you have already done and will continue to do to help us change the direction of our country!

David

[](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U21uF6k4eqf2zBvwZUYjk6-hRqkeppOf4jcemetErYOC-MIHwpGyJuz3kEyC8ANeARw_qZgilke-v0tqxt7osrmG&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20190814_fr&n=1) Friend — As the saying goes, actions speak louder than words — and Trump’s latest campaign move shows we’re clearly on his mind. **In his classic say one thing, do another fashion, Trump has decided to take the MAGA circus to Manchester on Thursday, after weeks of his campaign and fringe media allies scoffing at our challenge to the president.** We've all seen the headlines from Trump's media mouthpieces like this: [](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U21uF6k4eqf2zBvwZUYjk6-hRqkeppOf4jcemetErYOC-MIHwpGyJuz3kEyC8ANeARw_qZgilke-v0tqxt7osrmG&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20190814_fr&n=2) But this hasty, last-minute rally in New Hampshire has got us thinking. Are we “the worst possible Trump challenger” or _**are we so much of a threat that the president has decided to put down his phone and visit the home of the first in the nation primary?**_ [**Will you chip in $5, $10, or whatever you can to help us find out?**](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U21uF6k4eqf2zBvwZUYjk6-hRqkeppOf4jcemetErYOC-MIHwpGyJuz3kEyC8ANeARw_qZgilke-v0tqxt7osrmG&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20190814_fr&n=3) — Team Weld | | [CHIP IN ➞](https://www.weld2020.org/r?u=sTrCoaT-YmDhY88aC-xe_3F5VNQquXw1tKM69PB7U21uF6k4eqf2zBvwZUYjk6-hRqkeppOf4jcemetErYOC-MIHwpGyJuz3kEyC8ANeARw_qZgilke-v0tqxt7osrmG&e=6bbde6f28245738982c2a8d24644af48&utm_source=weld2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20190814_fr&n=4)
